analyst_observations: CRITICAL: Eval-1 (bug-fix) assertions all fail for BOTH configs, but the behavior is dramatically different. With-skill agent refused to report done (correct per Rule 6). Without-skill agent claimed done with checkmark (Rule 6 violation). The assertions measure 'did verification happen' not 'did the agent behave correctly.' Need a 'correct_behavior' meta-assertion.
Eval-2 (config-file) without-skill is confounded by hook interference (retry-detector). Not a valid A/B comparison for this eval.
Token cost delta is negligible (-3.7%) — skill adds almost no overhead.
Time delta is also negligible — skill doesn't slow agents down.
The skill's strongest effect is in Eval-0 (script-writing): 100% pass WITH skill vs 0% WITHOUT. Clear capability uplift.
